reforms.description: Japan is promoting flagship projects to realize 'Society 5.0', which includes the implementation of automated mobility systems, data-driven healthcare, and smart energy management, aimed at enhancing productivity and sustainability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These projects are expected to drive technological advancements and improve productivity across various sectors, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Commercial services for 5G and other technologies began in 2020.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift towards integrating advanced technologies into society, aiming for comprehensive economic and social transformation.
reforms.source_quote: Promote such projects as: i) automated mobility systems; ii) data-driven healthcare systems; iii) smart energy management and finance.

reforms.description: A framework for project-based regulatory sandboxes was created to foster innovation by allowing businesses to test new products and services in a controlled environment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This framework encourages experimentation and innovation, which can lead to new business models and technologies that enhance economic growth.
reforms.importance_rationale: The creation of regulatory sandboxes is a significant step towards fostering innovation, allowing for more agile responses to technological advancements.
reforms.source_quote: A framework for project-based regulatory sandboxes was created in 2018.

reforms.description: Japan will grant preferential treatment in public procurement for advanced technology and SMEs to stimulate innovation and support small businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform is expected to enhance the competitiveness of SMEs and promote the adoption of innovative technologies in public services.
reforms.status_evidence: Preferential treatment in public procurement was established.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant for enhancing the role of SMEs in the innovation ecosystem, promoting economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: Preferential treatment in public procurement will be granted for advanced technology and SMEs and venture businesses.

reforms.description: The New Economic Policy Package announced at the end of 2017 aims to double productivity growth to 2% by 2020, focusing on the integration of digital technologies and addressing labor shortages.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By integrating digital technologies, the policy is expected to enhance productivity and create new business opportunities, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The policy package was announced and is being executed.
reforms.importance_rationale: This policy represents a significant shift towards leveraging digital technologies for economic growth, addressing critical labor market challenges, and enhancing productivity.
reforms.source_quote: The New Economic Policy Package announced at end-2017 set a goal of doubling productivity growth to 2% by 2020.

reforms.description: The regulatory sandbox system introduced in 2018 allows projects using new technology to be exempt from existing regulations under certain conditions, facilitating innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers for innovative projects, the initiative encourages experimentation and the development of new technologies, which can drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulatory sandbox system was introduced and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it creates a more favorable environment for innovation, although it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The regulatory sandbox system introduced in 2018, which exempts projects using new technology from existing regulations under certain conditions, is a welcome step forward.

reforms.description: In 2018, Japan revised the guideline for strategic basic technology advancement to expand support for R&D projects related to AI and IoT between SMEs and universities and government research institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing R&D collaboration between SMEs and research institutions can lead to innovation and improved productivity in the SME sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The guideline was revised and its coverage expanded.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the framework for R&D collaboration, targeting key technological areas that are crucial for SME growth and innovation.
reforms.source_quote: In 2018, the guideline for the strategic basic technology advancement/collaboration support projects was revised and its coverage expanded to include R&D projects related to AI and IoT between SMEs and universities and government research institutes.

reforms.description: Japan is promoting the development of technologies to improve power generation efficiency and drastically reduce GHG emissions, including through carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing power generation efficiency and reducing emissions can lead to sustainable economic growth and innovation in energy technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively promoting these technologies as part of its energy strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards sustainable energy practices and innovation in Japan's energy sector, aligning with global climate commitments.
reforms.source_quote: Japan is promoting the development of technologies to improve power generation efficiency and drastically reduce GHG emissions, in part through carbon capture, utilisation and storage (CCUS).

reforms.description: The government aims to create a 'hydrogen society' by producing hydrogen-fuelled cars and promoting hydrogen use across various sectors.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Developing hydrogen technologies can lead to new markets and job creation while contributing to decarbonisation efforts.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to create a 'hydrogen society'.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant commitment to developing a new energy sector, although it is still in the planning stages.
reforms.source_quote: The government aims to create a 'hydrogen society'.

reforms.description: Osaka Prefecture has established a Structural Reform Special Zone to promote the creation of a biomedical innovation cluster, which includes lifting restrictions on businesses run by university professors to enhance collaboration between academia and industry.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative fosters collaboration between academia and industry, leading to advancements in medical technology and increased firm entry rates.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has resulted in important advances in medical technology and increased firm entry rates.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at fostering innovation and collaboration in the biomedical sector, with potential long-term economic benefits.
reforms.source_quote: In 2004, the national government approved Osaka Prefecture’s proposal to designate a district in its northern area as a Structural Reform Special Zone to promote the creation of a biomedical innovation cluster.

reforms.description: Osaka Prefecture has implemented local special zones that offer significant tax reductions for approved life science or alternative energy businesses, enhancing the region's attractiveness for startups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing corporate income tax rates, these zones incentivize business formation and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: These initiatives have helped lift Osaka’s firm entry rate above Tokyo’s and the national average.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant in promoting local economic development and innovation, though it is part of a broader strategy rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: Osaka Prefecture promotes growth through local special zones in which the local corporate income tax rate is cut to as low as zero for approved life science or alternative energy businesses.

reforms.description: Japan will launch a system of professional and vocational universities (PVU) and professional and vocational junior colleges (PVJC) to enhance vocational training and cooperation with industry, addressing the skills gap in the labor market.
reforms.package_name: 2018-22 National Basic Plan for the Promotion of Education
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances the skills of the workforce, aligning education with industry needs, which can boost productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The system is set to launch in April 2019.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Japan's approach to vocational education, aiming to better align skills training with labor market demands, which is crucial for economic adaptation.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2019, Japan will launch a system of professional and vocational universities (PVU) and professional and vocational junior colleges (PVJC).

reforms.description: The government launched the PFI/PPP Action Plan in 2016, aiming to enhance infrastructure investment and maintenance efficiency through public-private partnerships, with a target of JPY 21 trillion in projects over FY 2013-22.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By leveraging private finance initiatives, the plan aims to improve the efficiency of public infrastructure projects, which can lead to better service delivery and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Projects have met their goals in most targeted areas.
reforms.importance_rationale: This action plan represents a significant shift towards utilizing private sector capabilities in public infrastructure, which is crucial for addressing Japan's ageing infrastructure challenges.
reforms.source_quote: The government launched the PFI/PPP Action Plan in 2016, which envisaged projects of JPY 21 trillion (3.8% of 2018 GDP) over FY 2013-22.

reforms.description: The 2018 amendments of the Act on Promotion of Private Finance Initiative incentivize local governments to introduce concessions for water supply and sewerage, enhancing public-private collaboration in infrastructure.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These amendments aim to improve the efficiency and sustainability of public services through private sector involvement, which can lead to better resource allocation and service delivery.
reforms.status_evidence: The amendments provided local governments incentives to introduce concessions for water supply and sewerage.
reforms.importance_rationale: The amendments represent a significant enhancement of existing frameworks for public-private partnerships, particularly in essential services like water supply.
reforms.source_quote: The 2018 amendments of the Act on Promotion of Private Finance Initiative gave local governments incentives to introduce concessions for water supply and sewerage.
